c1.speaker: milo
c1.line: I can't tell you. Because then YOU'D know I was out at two in the morning, and then MOM would know, and journalism has ETHICS, I read about it.

c2.speaker: gary
c2.line: Trade you. A secret for a secret.

c3.speaker: gary
c3.line: I'm scared of grapes, Milo. Not scared — it's... we have a complicated history. Nobody knows that.

c6.speaker: milo
c6.line: Two nights before the ceremony. A tall figure crossed the square. I couldn't see his face — couldn't see ears or antlers or ANYTHING, the umbrella was in the way of the whole him. But I saw the umbrella. THE umbrella. The fancy Vale one with the silver duck handle. Nobody else has that umbrella.
